inputs.titre: Début de la culture intensive du houblon en Europe centrale pour la production de bière

reason: The image depicts a hop field in central Europe around the 9th century. Farmers are wearing woolen cloaks and are working the hop vines, which are supported by rough-hewn wood structures. The muddy ground and the dense forest indicate a rural setting consistent with the early stages of hop cultivation. There are no blatant anachronisms or fantastical elements. The presence of simple tools and clothing suggests a rustic and manual labor-intensive environment, fitting for the time. A potential inaccuracy could be the seeming modernity of the farming techniques, as hop cultivation methods in the 9th century might not have been as organized or efficient. However, overall the picture aligns well with the described event.
